ab: kapah mipalita kaku tisuwan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: kapah tu, mahang tu kaku.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: taneng kaku mipalita tisuwan haw ?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: amana, caay kayadah ku katineng aku.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: namalahuk silawad kisu? maydih mizizaw kaku tisuwan taneng tu?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: hang i, halhalen aku kisu i luma’.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: ancila silawad kisu haw? maydih mipalita kaku tisuwan tu sakay Sakizaya a kungku.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於撒奇萊雅族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Sakizaya tribe.

ab: a tabuyu’ kaku ancila, caay ka i luma’.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: u tengil aku u satinengay tu nu Sakizaya a lisin nu babalaki kisu, madih mipalita tisuwan.
ch: A:我聽說您對撒奇萊雅的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the customs of Sakizaya and would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: caay kayadah ku katinengan aku, nikabilil han tu nu maku mipatubeli.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: hani kapah miliadingu kaku haw? mahiza anayaw kya madayum micilu.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: hang i, nayi’ ku sapabaliw.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: hani pinaay tu ku mihcaan isu?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: siwa bataan izaw ku walu ku mihcaan aku.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: masakapahay henay kisu.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: i cuwa saw! u balakiay tu kaku.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: hani namakay cuwaay kisu?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: makay Takubuwan kaku.
en: B: I’m from Dagupu Bay.

ab: hani u Bunung ku acawa isu?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是布農族嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Bunun?

ab: caay, u Sakizaya ciniza.
en: B: No, she’s a Sakizaya.

ab: pina ku katenengan isu a kamu?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: matineng mihanu Sakizaya atu Pangcah a kamu kaku.
ch: B: 我會說撒奇萊雅語和阿美語。
en: B: I speak Sakizaya and Pangcah.

ab: ayza a demiad manamuh mipalita tisuwan, sakamu wamay kisu, sakay miadupay a paysin haw?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: hang i. namasakapah henay kaku hina tabuyu’ miadup.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: kukay, ayza kapah kuni pasubana’an isu.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: nakamuwan aku u tatenga’an amin, nakamuwan nu babalaki.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: ayza a demiad hatini tu ku nipalita, kukay kuni pawapen isu.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: amana kasikaz, masasakamu haw tu kita.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: a mumul tu kaku, ayaw han tu a matineng tu yadahay a kungku.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: kapah tu! haymaw han tu.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
